Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deft'S Document Production And Further Responses To Interrogatories;
Off Calendar Jun-08-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: Plaintiff Danny K. Paul's Motion To Compel Defendant's Document Production And Further Responses To Interrogatories is granted. As to the form interrogatories, the response to No. 4.1 is incomplete. The objections to Numbers 13.1 and 13.2 are overruled and ordered stricken. Defendant is ordered to respond further and to produce documents within 20 days of notice of this order.

As to the Special interrogatories, the motion is granted as to Numbers 6, 7, 10, 13. The objections are overruled and ordered stricken.

As to Numbers 8 and 9 the objections are overruled and ordered stricken. Defendant is ordered to respond further, limited to documented formal training. As to No. 12, Defendant is ordered to identify the Safety Officer.

Further responses to the Special Interrogatories shall be made within 20 days of notice of this order.

The motion is granted as to the Request For Documents Numbers 2, 3, 6, 7, 8, 9, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, 17, and 18. The objections are overruled and ordered stricken. Defendant shall respond and produce further within 20 days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED.

Notice Of Motion Andmotion To Quash Deposition Subpoenas For Production Of Business Records And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions;
Off Calendar Nov-21-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Steger P. Johnson, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: Danny Paul
Galin G. Luk, Esq., for Defendant and opposing party: Tutor-Saliba, et al

HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: Plaintiff Danny K. Paul's Motion To Quash Defendant's Deposition Subpoena for Production Of Business Records is granted. Plaintiff has a right to privacy as to his academic records. Defendant has failed to show good cause to invade that privacy. The records lack sufficient probative value, the subpoenas are overly broad, and they seek records overly remote in time. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED

Notice Of Deft'S Motion And Motion To Compel The Pltf'S Document Production And Response To Form And Special Interrogatories
Off Calendar Nov-19-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: Defendant Tutor-Saliba/Koch/Tidewater JV's Motion To Compel Plaintiff's Production Of Documents And Response To Form And Special Interrogatories is moot as amended responses have been served. The court will not determine the adequacy of those responses on this motion. Counsel are ordered to meet and confer on those amended responses. Counsel are advised that Interrogatory Nos. 22 and 30 are overly broad. Counsel are also advised that there is a tax return privilege. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED
